title: Fluoride ions as charge carriers in electrochemical energy storage
abstract: Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02654-y Lithium-ion batteries are commonplace, but large-scale production leads to potential materials criticality constraints. This Review discusses fluoride-anion systems as a potential alternative, outlining design principles for electrodes, materials design concepts, degradation mechanisms and research fronts that must be considered.

title: Quantifying Capacity Loss Modes of Li Metal Anodes Upon Deep Stripping in Primary Batteries with Fluorinated Catholytes
authors: Alejandro R. Sevilla, Kyle S. Jiang, Gi Hyun Byun, Stuart Hellring, Haining Gao and Betar M. Gallant
abstract: Fluorinated catholytes based on pentafluorosulfanyl arenes offer a promising route to high-performance <mark>lithium</mark> (Li) primary batteries and can be integrated into cells with conventional carbon monofluoride (CFx) cathodes to markedly extend the <mark>energy density</mark> of Li–CFx batteries. Previously, the catholyte-derived Li anode <mark>solid electrolyte</mark> <mark>interphase</mark> (SEI) was observed to impede high-rate discharge at 25 °C compared to excellent <mark>rate capability</mark> at elevated temperature (50 °C). Meanwhile, the accessible Li anode capacity upon full stripping, critical for practical applications, has not been evalu…

title: Low-Potential Enzymatic Glucose Sensor with Wide Linear Range Based on Nitrogen-Doped Reduced Graphene Oxide
authors: Ji Yoon Lee and Yong-Cheol Jeong
abstract: Reduced graphene oxide (rGO) is gaining attention as a material for electrochemical sensors due to its high surface area, versatile functional groups, and excellent biocompatibility. In this study, we developed an enzymatic glucose sensor by integrating nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide (N-rGO) onto a commercial platinum screen-printed electrode (Pt SPE) through a facile chemical reduction approach using hydrazine, which simultaneously reduces graphene oxide and introduces nitrogen dopants. Graphite was highly oxidized to produce graphene oxide with abundant functional groups suitable for …

title: Underpotential-Mediated Co-Deposition of Copper-Manganese from an Alkaline Electrolyte for Application in Semiconductor Interconnects
authors: Madaline Oakes, Nicholas Sinclair, Christian Kellamis and Rohan Akolkar
abstract: Manganese (Mn) doping is a promising route for enhancing the electromigration resistance of copper (Cu) interconnects. However, conventional electrodeposition of Cu-Mn is hindered by the large difference in the reduction potentials between Cu and Mn, as well as the parasitic hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) which leads to a porous deposit morphology. To circumvent these issues, we present here a novel electrochemical strategy leveraging Mn underpotential deposition (UPD) to co-deposit a Cu-Mn alloy from a shelf-stable citrate-based alkaline electrolyte. We demonstrate precise control over bot…

title: Transport-Limited Interfacial Electrochemistry in Porous Electrodes
authors: Xiaoyu Yang and Daniel M. Tartakovsky
abstract: Fast charge and discharge of <mark>lithium</mark> batteries, i.e., high C-rates, induce diffusion-dominated <mark>lithium</mark> transport in the active material, with strong intra-particle concentration gradients significantly impacting the interfacial electrochemistry. In this regime, conventional porous-electrode models struggle to reproduce the experimentally measured lithiation curves, such that discrepancy between the model predictions and the observations increases with C-rate. We present a dual-continuum porous-electrode model, which retains its prediction accuracy in both reaction- and transport-limited regime…
